data.title: ECB wants to build blockchain-based payment system
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Tree News, the European Central Bank hopes to establish a blockchain-based payment system.

data.title: SEC announces creation of Cyber ​​and Emerging Technologies Unit to replace Crypto Assets and Cyber ​​Unit
European Central Bank Explores Blockchain-Based Payment System
data.description: According to Foresight News, the European Central Bank is considering the development of a payment system based on blockchain technology. This initiative aims to enhance the efficiency and security of financial transactions within the Eurozone. The move reflects a growing interest in leveraging blockchain to modernize traditional banking systems and improve cross-border payment processes.

data.title: SEC Establishes New Division To Safeguard Retail Investors
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) announced on February 20 the creation of a new division focused on cybersecurity and emerging technologies. This initiative aims to enhance the protection of retail investors. The establishment of this division underscores the SEC's commitment to addressing the challenges posed by technological advancements in the financial sector.

data.title: The three major U.S. stock indexes opened lower
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: At the opening of the U.S. stock market, the Dow fell 177 points, the S&P 500 fell 0.23%, and the Nasdaq fell 0.14%. Walmart (WMT.N) fell 4.7%, and its earnings guidance for fiscal year 2026 fell short of expectations. Alibaba (BABA.N) rose 9%, and the company's financial report exceeded market expectations. Alibaba's IDC partner GDS (GDS.O) rose 19%, and 21Vianet (VNET.O) rose 16%. Bilibili (BILI.O) rose 12%, and the company turned a profit in the fourth quarter of 2024. The Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rose 3.2%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 98,000 USDT with a 1.46% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 20, 2025, 14:35 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 98,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 98,014 USDT, with a narrowed 1.46% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Fixed Income: A Missing Component In DeFi's Evolution
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector has seen rapid growth, yet it still lacks a crucial financial element: fixed income. In a recent discussion on the podcast 'Hashing It Out,' Elisha Owusu Akyaw conversed with Brandon Goh, co-founder and CEO of Treehouse, about the significance of fixed income for DeFi's long-term stability and its potential to attract institutional investors.

Fixed income, encompassing assets like bonds and savings accounts, is a cornerstone of traditional finance but has not gained significant traction in the crypto space. Goh points out that while DeFi has developed derivatives, lending platforms, and decentralized exchanges, it lacks a foundational benchmark rate similar to the London Interbank Offered Rate (LIBOR). The absence of standardized rates poses a challenge to scaling fixed-income products within a decentralized framework. Goh explains that the industry has overlooked an entire asset class, rendering it nearly non-existent in the current DeFi landscape. He emphasizes the need for missing infrastructure, particularly benchmark rates, to establish a robust fixed income asset class.

To address this gap, Goh suggests the creation of a decentralized offered rate (DOR), an onchain benchmark aimed at enhancing market efficiency and transparency. Treehouse's model, as highlighted by Goh, enables users to earn predictable returns while managing risks associated with yield-generating products. The conversation further delves into how institutions might enter the DeFi space. Goh argues that major financial entities are unlikely to engage in high-risk DeFi strategies but may begin with stable, transparent yield opportunities such as staking. A dependable fixed-income market could serve as a gateway, facilitating the integration of traditional finance with the crypto world.

Goh anticipates that DeFi will shift towards sustainable growth, moving away from unsustainable high-yield incentives. As regulatory clarity improves and infrastructure develops, fixed income could become a fundamental aspect of DeFi's next evolutionary phase. This discussion sheds light on a vital yet underdeveloped area of DeFi that could significantly influence the industry's future as it aims for mainstream acceptance.
